name: Paraguay
avoid: December to February is the hottest, muggiest stretch, and the Chaco can feel brutally dry and above 40°C in the afternoon.
Heavy rain can slow Chaco road travel and make long self-drive days tiring, especially if you are trying to connect Filadelfia with eastern Paraguay in one shot.
Ciudad del Este border traffic around Foz do Iguaçu can be chaotic on weekends and holidays, so keep buffers before flights or long bus legs.
landmark: Jesuit Missions of La Santísima Trinidad de Paraná
stopoverNote: Fits neatly with Iguazú / Foz do Iguaçu, or with Argentina's Misiones province if you want a wider Jesuit-history loop.

cities.name: Asunción
cities.notes: Historic center for the Panteón, riverfront walks, traditional bars, and the easiest entry point for buses, taxis, and rental cars.

cities.name: Encarnación
cities.notes: Paraná river promenade base for Playa San José and the best launch point for the Jesuit missions at Trinidad and Jesús.

cities.name: Ciudad del Este
cities.notes: Busy Triple Frontier city for Itaipu, shopping, and easy onward links to Foz do Iguaçu plus nearby natural viewpoints.

cities.name: Chaco (Filadelfia)
cities.notes: Dry western Paraguay stop for Mennonite museums, broad-road Chaco landscapes, and birding or national-park style day drives.

itinerary.sim: Tigo and Personal prepago are the easiest tourist SIM choices; expect roughly ₹550–₹1,100 for a practical short-stay data pack. Signal is reliable in Asunción, Encarnación, and Ciudad del Este, but download maps before long Chaco drives because coverage becomes patchy outside Filadelfia.

Asunción.name: Asunción
Asunción.note: Stay in Villa Morra or the historic center if you want easier taxi hops between museums, restaurants, and the riverside. Asunción is compact enough for short taxi rides, but the center is best explored on foot in the morning before the heat builds.

activities.name: Panteón Nacional de los Héroes and surrounding Palma Street walk
Casa de la Independencia and Palacio de López exterior circuit
Centro Cultural El Cabildo or Museo Casa de la Independencia stop
Golden-hour stroll on the Costanera de Asunción

hotels.name: Palmaroga Hotel Asuncion, Tapestry Collection by Hilton

activities.name: Parque Ñu Guasú walking or cycling loop
Taxi ride between central Asunción and Ñu Guasú / airport side
Museo del Barro for Paraguayan folk and contemporary art
Sunset drinks or dinner around Villa Morra and Paseo La Galería

Encarnación.name: Encarnación
Encarnación.note: Encarnación works best with one city day and one missions day. Stay near the Costanera so you can walk the waterfront, then decide between a taxi/driver or rental car for the Jesuit ruins loop.

days.theme: Costanera, Playa San José & Riverside Evening

activities.name: Costanera de Encarnación walk and Playa San José break
Plaza de Armas and Catedral de Encarnación
Short taxi hops between the waterfront, downtown, and hotel zone
Evening riverfront cafés and the Sambódromo surroundings
Jesuit Missions of La Santísima Trinidad de Paraná and Jesús de Tavarangue combined ticket
Taxi, private driver, or rental-car run from Encarnación to the mission sites
UNESCO site walks through the main plazas, church façades, and mission remains

Ciudad del Este.name: Ciudad del Este
Ciudad del Este.note: Base near the downtown river side or closer to Hernandarias if Itaipu is your priority. Traffic can be intense, so start early for Itaipu and keep border-crossing plans separate from the sightseeing day.

activities.name: Itaipu Binacional panoramic visitor tour
Taxi or app ride between central Ciudad del Este and Itaipu visitor center in Hernandarias
Costanera Hernandarias or nearby river viewpoints after the dam visit
Evening food stop back in Ciudad del Este

hotels.name: Rio Hotel by Bourbon Ciudad del Este

activities.name: Templo Budista Chen Tien visit for hillside city views
Saltos del Monday nature stop in Presidente Franco
Taxi circuit or half-day rental car for Presidente Franco and downtown shopping districts
Shopping del Este or Monalisa area browse if you want a lighter afternoon

Chaco (Filadelfia).name: Chaco (Filadelfia)
Chaco (Filadelfia).note: Filadelfia is best with a rental car because distances are longer and the best Chaco stops are spread out. Keep fuel topped up, carry water, and front-load outdoor visits into the cooler morning hours.

activities.name: Museo Histórico y de Historia Natural Fernheim
Museo Jakob Unger
Monumento de la Paz del Chaco and central Filadelfia walk
Local taxi or short self-drive hops around Filadelfia

days.theme: Chaco Nature Drive or Teniente Enciso Day Trip

activities.name: Rental car day for birding roads, estancia scenery, or the long-run option toward Parque Nacional Teniente Enciso
Slow tereré and café stop back in Filadelfia after the drive

connections.method: Intercity bus from the Terminal de Ómnibus de Asunción is the standard 5.5–6.5 hr run; taxis are only practical to the terminal, while a rental car gives flexibility for stops on Ruta PY01
Intercity buses cover the 4–5 hr eastern hop; a rental car is faster if you want to add small stops between Itapúa and Alto Paraná
Long-distance bus is the usual 5–6 hr reset before heading west; private transfers are expensive, so most travelers either bus or self-drive
Daily intercity buses and rental cars both work for the 5–6 hr Trans-Chaco leg, but a rental car is much more useful once you start exploring beyond Filadelfia town
Taxi, rideshare, or short cross-border bus links make Ciudad del Este the easiest Paraguay add-on from the wider Iguazú region

extras: May to September is the sweet spot because days are drier and cooler, which matters most in Asunción afternoons and even more on Chaco road trips.
The Jesuit missions ticket covers both Trinidad and Jesús, so try to leave Encarnación early enough to enjoy the sites before the harshest midday heat.
Itaipu's standard panoramic tour is free, but reserve ahead when possible because slots can fill on weekends and holiday periods.
Keep small cash reserves for buses, kiosks, and simpler Chaco stops even though cards are widely accepted in bigger hotels and restaurants in the east.
If you are short on time, keep Asunción plus Encarnación as the cultural core, add Ciudad del Este for Itaipu, and treat Filadelfia as the deeper overland extension.

Asunción: Panteón Nacional de los Héroes Asunción
Ciudad del Este: Templo Budista Chen Tien Ciudad del Este
